001.text: The ship malfunctions! You have mere seconds to escape.Each player gains a different skill (select or randomize) and places the matching location in their location card holder:002003004005006007After players simultaneously follow the instructions on their unique escape pod, each player should have 4 skill tokens (1 from step 2 on location 000[/b], 1 from location 001[/b], and 2 from their escape pod); the first player should have 2 extra skill tokens from choosing the mission. Each player should also have 2 boost on their character.

1002.text: Succeed if the order of the circuit boards is 1-D-B-C-A-6. If you succeed, gain a fully functional Mask of Memory (item 1141[/b]) (also, if you have it, lose the circuit curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose item 1002[/b] and the puzzle cards.

110.text: Succeed if the numbers highlighted in green from left to right match the flower heights of your arrangement: 1/2/4. If you succeed, gain item 1248[/b], an emblem representative of the leaf sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the leaf curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 1135[/b].

125.text: Succeed if you chose Tunnel 2 (or found another complete path from bottom to top). If you succeed, gain the Volcanic Scepter (item 998[/b]) and perform no other location actions here. If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 532[/b]. Your location is still 125[/b].

128.text: Succeed if you force the knight into a pit. If so, the knight pledges their loyalty to you if you let them keep the blade (gain sentient 1357[/b]). Alternatively, you can reject their offer of loyalty and gain the blade as your prize (gain item 1005[/b]). If you fail by ending up on the same square, lose 1 health. Succeed or fail, lose card 533[/b]. Your location is still 128[/b], but you may not perform any other location actions here.

1577.text: You suit up into your power armor. Replace your character with the corresponding card (captain[/b]: 1447[/b]; navigator[/b]: 1448[/b]; scholar[/b]: 1449[/b]; medic[/b]: 1450[/b]; engineer[/b]: 1451[/b]; marine[/b]: 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card.

1578.text: You gently swaddle the ship’s cats. Gain animal 1122[/b]; the cats are safe with you.

1579.text: You hold your head high, having reached your full potential. Now you’re ready for anything… or maybe you’ve done enough. You may either gain destiny 1598[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1580.text: Your collection of flora would make any galactic botanist green with envy. You may either gain destiny 1607[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1581.text: You scan the planet for dense centers of civilization, and the result is around a dozen cities scattered across the world. Gain item 1251[/b].

1582.text: Your quick wit, charisma, and generosity of spirit have helped you assemble a lively group of sentients. You may either gain destiny 1605[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1583.text: Your valuable collection will fetch a pretty penny at the galactic market. You may either gain destiny 1601[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1584.text: You are surrounded with an array of curious animals. You may either gain destiny 1602[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1585.text: You have completed the mission of charting this unknown planet—you will be hailed as a true pioneer by the Galactic Cartographers Guild! Now you can put some of that knowledge to use by transmitting a rescue message to your homeworld. Gain destiny 1598[/b] (place it in the center); each player also gains 1 random skill. If any player is currently on a location with an engage-TRANSMIT[/b][/b] action, on their next turn they may perform that action. Alternatively, you may end the game in a mission victory.

1586.text: You are proud of your altruism here on this alien planet. You may either gain destiny 1603[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1587.text: Your ship’s benefactors will be elated by the riches you’ve earned on this planet. You may either gain destiny 1600[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1588.text: You feel an immense sense of satisfaction for completing your personal journey through a series of three objectives. You may either gain destiny 1599[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1589.text: You swipe a crewmate’s personal item when they’re not looking. Gain another character’s EQUIP[/b] result (whether or not they are in this game): captain’s saber[/b] (1299[/b]), navigator’s binoculars[/b] (1300[/b]), scholar’s notebook[/b] (1301[/b]), medic’s first-aid kit[/b] (1302[/b]), engineer’s multi‑tool[/b] (1303[/b]), or marine’s axe[/b] (1304[/b]).

1590.text: Ignore the safety instructions about entering a separate escape pod. Instead, choose one of the following:Select another player and join them in their escape pod (you each gain the associated skill).Gain an unused character card to your reserve (1435[/b]–1440[/b]). You can later add them to your grid, but not to replace your character (you are your character).

1591.text: You wipe sweat from your brow, tired but full of hope that this shelter will be the first of many for your people on this planet. You may either gain destiny 1601[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1592.text: You waged war on this planet, and your new seat of power is this city. You may either gain destiny 1602[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1593.text: You came a long way to connect with the people of this planet, and hopefully these new bonds will make the galaxy a better place for everyone. You may either gain destiny 1605[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1594.text: After your corporation sees the power of these crystals, they will set up a mining operation here. You may either gain destiny 1607[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1595.text: You have mustered the firepower necessary to liberate your homeworld. You may either gain destiny 1606[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1596.text: You are excited by the opportunities this planet offers. Choose your destiny:

choices.outcome: Gain destiny 1600[/b]; place it in the center.

choices.option: Start a business on this planet
choices.outcome: Gain destiny 1601[/b]; place it in the center.
Gain destiny 1603[/b]; place it in the center.

1597.text: You are elated that such a precious item is now yours. You may either gain destiny 1604[/b] to pursue an epic victory via it or any other destiny (place it in the center and each player gains 1 random skill; ignore actions and instructions on the mission from now on) or end the game in a mission victory.

1598.text: As the signal beams into the great beyond, you reminisce about your time on this planet. Perhaps someday you will return. Within a few hours, an intergalactic rescue team arrives, and soon the planet is but a distant speck on your monitor. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1599.text: Reunited at last, you embrace each other and don’t let go for a long time, despite how bad you all smell. Your crew and your cats are your family, your home. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1600.text: At long last, you have a roof over your head that you can call your own. You look forward to the day when you can host your crewmates and tell stories about the good ol’ days. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1601.text: Entrepreneurs at heart, you’re proud to get your hands dirty every day to serve your customers and earn your keep on this planet. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1602.text: Flying through sky and space on a dragon is exhilarating—it defies everything you know about physics. You feel a new sense of freedom and empowerment, and you look forward to the adventures that await you. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1603.text: Each of you have earned glory and renown, whether from helping a sentient in this world or winning a duel. Having achieved something important to you, you rest peacefully, knowing that you are enough. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1604.text: Wielding the power of space gives you immediate access to the entire world—nay, the entire universe! Nothing can stop you from gathering your friends and teleporting back home before starting your next adventure. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1605.text: It takes time, skill, effort, and a little luck, but your ship is spaceworthy again. You take to the sky to find your crewmates before setting sail for your home planet. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1606.text: Step by step, vehicle by vehicle, you worked your way up into a spaceship that—if you can say so yourself—is even better than the original. It’s time to head home. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1607.text: Given the immense amount of time, labor, and materials necessary to build your ship from scratch, you are incredibly proud of your creation. The ship is far superior to the original, and you look forward to showing it off to the engineers back home. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

1608.text: With a snap of your fingers, you and your crew are transported back in time to the cockpit of the spaceship as it approaches the planet. You’re all surrounded by the various possessions you accumulated during your journey— they time traveled with you. You know what is about to happen, and you have a choice:

choices.option: Quickly steer the ship off course, preventing disaster (end the game)
choices.outcome: Baffled, the redshirts who perished in the original crash require an explanation. They are relieved you saved them from that fate, but you can tell they’re a bit envious of your adventure. Perhaps someday you’ll return together. The game ends, and you achieve an epic victory if you also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

choices.option: Let history repeat itself (start a new game, retaining the progress you already made)
choices.outcome: Keep grids, reserves, and skills exactly the same; lose all other cards. Go to location 000[/b] and start with step 3.

1609.text: When you were a child, you and your best friend entered a local cave. A sudden cave-in crushed your legs and killed your friend. You have always felt the guilt of survival, and you’ve stayed far away from caves. Now you crest the entrance, ready to show the deep dark that you are the captain of your fear. Lose objective 1609[/b], then gain 1 morale and objective 1615[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1610.text: You spent the entire trip to this planet either at the navigation console or with your head buried in a book, telling yourself that you’re better off expanding your knowledge than making friends. It’s a story you’ve told yourself since you were a kid. Crash landing on this planet came with some level of relief for you, as you’ve trained your entire life to be alone. You’re now experiencing new levels of focus that you could never have achieved with all the distractions of daily life. Lose objective 1610[/b], then gain 1 morale and objective 1616[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1611.text: Your first love was a girl in the elevator of your apartment complex. She smiled at you; you smiled back and said hello. When she replied in a different language, you repeated her version of the greeting until she nodded in approval. Every time you saw her in the elevator, you tried out a new word or phrase, always wanting to say more or extend the conversation. But it always ended when she slowly got off at the 322nd floor, lingering in case you had something to add, but you didn’t have the confidence. Lose objective 1611[/b], then gain 1 morale and objective 1617[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1612.text: You were a rebellious teenager, or at least you thought of yourself that way. It didn’t help that your father was incredibly patient with you. He didn’t wave it off as a phase or try to chastise you; rather, if you lashed out or broke curfew, he’d leave a flower on your desk. Before you left for college, you asked him about the flowers. He explained that it went back to the blanket you were wrapped in when he adopted you as an infant—it had a floral print. Flowers were his way of saying that he loved you unconditionally, forever. Lose objective 1612[/b], then gain 1 morale and objective 1618[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1613.text: You grew up on the streets of a megacity, homeless at a young age after being abandoned by your parents. At first you just barely scraped by, but you found a dry place to sleep and learned to steal. The rule that kept you alive was to steal only on the streets, never from any confined space with limited exits. The outside was your home, yet sometimes you’d catch yourself lingering too long at the windows of a warm bakery or home, wanting the comfort of walls and a roof. Lose objective 1613[/b], then gain 1 morale and objective 1619[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1614.text: You were born in a small, remote village: 200 people at most surrounded by endless, untouched wilderness. You learned to hunt soon after you learned to walk. Always for necessity, never for sport. Each part of the animals was used for the betterment of the community. Lose objective 1614[/b], then gain 1 morale and objective 1620[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1615.text: With your legs amputated following the cave-in, you spent your younger years at home, isolated and in pain from the prosthetics. You found refuge in online gaming, leading raids and rescue missions into fictional dungeons. Now you’ve embraced the opportunity to explore a new world, the type of place that you once only knew through virtual games. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, then gain objective 1621[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1616.text: Discovering the secrets of alien technology isn’t new for you. A few years back you spent the summer by yourself on an ice moon, studying an alien obelisk. You returned home to great acclaim and an invitation to a prestigious awards ceremony. You thought things would be different, walking into a room full of admiring colleagues, but even after a few drinks you couldn’t shake the feeling that you didn’t belong. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, then gain objective 1622[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1617.text: Your brush with young love was the catalyst for you to soak in new languages. You were driven to engage with other people from different cultures, backgrounds, and planets. Fortunately, your apartment complex was a melting pot of people from around the galaxy, so you had an abundance of opportunities. Despite your linguistic aptitude and a wide spectrum of friends, you still struggle with the confidence to make romantic connections. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, then gain objective 1623[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1618.text: Several years after you became a certified doctor, your father told you that he had intestinal lymphoma. You were halfway across the system at the time, but you talked through his options for treatment and called some doctors in the area to discuss any recent advances. A week later, you were by your father’s side when he entered surgery, a book of pressed flowers in hand. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, then gain objective 1624[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1619.text: As you grew up, you aged past the stage of petty theft and moved on to contract work. Hack jobs, heists, churn and burn: You tried it all, and you never made the same mistake twice. This led to a new rule, learned the hard way after two members of your crew betrayed you: Always work alone. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, then gain objective 1625[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1620.text: You grew up big, strong, and broad shouldered. When marine recruiters visited your settlement, you stood out. After spending your entire life with stars as your ceiling, you were suddenly living among them. You never forgot your roots, though. Whenever you’d drop onto a planet, you’d use your off hours to experience that world’s wild side, the places where nature was still in charge. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, then gain objective 1626[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

1621.text: The greatest day of your life was when you became the captain of your ship. You applied your natural and learned leadership skills to the academy, where you proudly walked the halls on prosthetic legs that you helped to design. Your ship was an extension of you and everything you achieved, so it is with great joy that you are now at the helm of a new vessel. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, replacing objective 1615[/b].

1622.text: This journey has helped you realize a few things about yourself: One, it’s okay that you find happiness in solitude, books, and research. Two, you need people in your life, just not all the time. And three, you can’t keep waiting for people to approach you. You have to make the first step, and you’re excited to try—and sometimes fail—in this city. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, replacing objective 1616[/b].

1623.text: You finally made the first move! Sure, it required you to crash land on a completely alien planet, but you did it! And they reciprocated! For all the connections you’ve felt speaking with people in a variety of languages over the years, this new connection trumps them all. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, replacing objective 1617[/b].

1624.text: Your father’s battle against cancer turned into an extended war. He lost his appetite and seemed thinner every time you saw him. Your confidence in modern medicine dropped, and you started to seek answers elsewhere, even in the arcane. One day you got the call you dreaded: The time had come to say goodbye. You were by his side at the end, holding his frail hand, telling him that you loved him unconditionally and that you were so grateful for him. Surrounded by flowers, he breathed his last breath. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, replacing objective 1618[/b].

1625.text: Despite your rule to work alone, you accepted a job on the ship. At first you viewed your crewmates as disposable—you wished them no ill will, but you vowed not to rely on them. After all, you’re here to steal the ship. So you watched from the outside as they became friends during the journey, eating together and playing games in the evening. Winning this duel made you realize that you could enjoy both worlds—within the context of a game, you can indulge your sneaky side while still connecting with the people around you. You didn’t have to stand outside anymore, hoping to be invited in. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, replacing objective 1619[/b].

1626.text: Whether you were hunting wild boars or corrupt warlords, you were perfectly comfortable with your bare hands. Yet something awakened in you the day you put on power armor for the first time. You felt whole. Conversely, when you took it off, you felt naked. That’s how you’ve felt since crashing on this world, and you’re overjoyed to be back in the armor. Rotate this card and add it to your grid, replacing objective 1620[/b].

1627.text: The Runekeeper rushes to their beloved dust mouse, gently stroking it between the ears. In gratitude, they offer to accompany you. Gain sentient 1329[/b] with 3 boost on them, gain $3, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1628.text: Their dignity restored by the victory, the Racer thanks you profusely and pledges their loyalty to you. Gain $2 and fill the Racer with boost, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1629.text: The bandit hideout (interior 563[/b]) is vacant, and you find a hidden drawer under the bed. Inside is a small vial containing a golden substance. This substance is the cure the wind sentients were looking for. Gain item 865[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1630.text: You are at an extreme elevation, able to see far around you in all directions. View page 38 of the Book of Vantages and gain 2 boost as you look down at the world below, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1631.text: You have successfully charted a small part of a vast ocean, and the Cartographer is pleased (yet even more curious than before). They give you a map of the various towers across the world. Gain item 1259[/b] and $2; the sentient highlights your current location (find your region in the look[/b] storybook, then view page 16 in the Book of Vantages). Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1632.text: A local thanks you for providing a safe option for travel to the north, and they press a few coins into your palm. Gain $10, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1633.text: The sinew elemental tears away from the tether, roaring in pleasure. Sinew sentients cower in embarrassment; it is their responsibility to keep their elemental tethered, and the fire sentients have made a mockery of them. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1634.text: The fisherman is elated to find the treasure chest intact. They produce a key, and from the chest they eagerly extract… a frying pan. Eyeing you with a mix of suspicion and appreciation, they hand over an egg as a reward and slowly backs away. Gain item 977[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1635.text: As life leaves the body of the stone sentient, you feel a wave of energy wash over you. Soon after, the sentient who gave you this quest gives you an appreciative hug. Gain sentient 1334[/b] (if you don’t already have them) and 1 morale, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1636.text: Busy with patrons, the exhausted tavern owner ushers you behind the bar. Before you know it, you’re serving a variety of fruit-based cocktails and oak-aged whiskey to various wood sentients. At closing time, the owner hands you the keys and gives you a big hug. Gain structure 1277[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1637.text: As you leave the Founder’s Temple, the energy sentient thanks you profusely. Their warmth immediately accelerates the hatching process; add 1 boost to the egg. Seeing that the egg is in good hands with you, they request that you care for it until it hatches. Gain quest 1679[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply), then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1638.text: Seeing you holding the package at the harbor (location 771[/b]), a merchant sidles next to you and furtively hands you a bag of coins. Lose item 1034[/b] (if you have it) and gain $10, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1639.text: The inventor is incredibly grateful for your rescue efforts; gain sentient 1356[/b] with 3 boost on them. The sentient’s eyes fill with salty tears when they hear that you came at the behest of their friend. They were imprisoned after learning that one of their inventions—a device that allowed water sentients to walk on land—was to be used for war instead of diplomacy. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1640.text: You are delighted to solve the mysteries of the Tools of Creation. Gain 1 morale, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid. If you are on location 666[/b] in the future and you still have the Tools of Creation, you may perform the overpower-CUT[/b][/b] action.

1641.text: The sentient wrenches the barrel from your grasp. They weigh it carefully in silence. For each boost token on the barrel, gain $5. Lose item 1158[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1642.text: The quick progress they’ve made with your assistance has elated the water sentients, and they promise to remember the favor. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1643.text: The child rushes into their parents’ arms, regaling them with stories from their adventure. Their mother and father express their appreciation for you and insist that you join them for dinner. Gain $3, 1 morale, and 1 health, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1644.text: When you present the item to the fire sentients, they are in awe of your crafting skills and insist that you keep the item. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1645.text: If you accused the fisherman of murdering Timber the repairman, you are correct! Timber had dominated the local fishing contest in recent years, unknowingly embarrassing the fisherman. To eliminate their top competitor, the fisherman used Timber’s own pike to slit their throat quietly. The other two suspects’ alibis involved loud activities, and Timber’s partner hadn’t told anyone what the fatal wound looked like. The fisherman’s key mistake was revealing that they knew the murder weapon. If you made an accurate accusation, gain either of the innocent sentients (the recruiter is 1359[/b] and the shipwright is 1364[/b]); if you already have either of them, fill them with boost. Whether or not you solved the mystery correctly, rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1647.text: You overcome the tyrant, telling them that their actions have consequences. No longer will they siphon from unwilling energy sentients. Gain 1 boost, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1648.text: The metal sentient is somewhat surprised to see you. “You stole the aether lantern and actually came back to give it to me? I’m impressed by your ethical flexibility!” They give you a pulse gun (gain item 1038[/b]) in appreciation for the lantern. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1649.text: The sinew hunters celebrate the successful hunt with hugs that almost crush you, declaring you a lifelong friend. If you still have sentient 1333[/b], you may either keep them or lose them to gain a skullhammer (item 950[/b]). Either way, rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1650.text: The stone sentient emerges from the mist, bounding toward their beloved hovertail. “The Leap of Faith worked!” they exclaim, referring to the ritual of honoring something or someone important to you (often with a wish attached to the daring act). They press some coins into your palm and insist that you visit them again someday. Gain $5, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1651.text: The three-headed sand sentient appears beside you as you gaze upon the constellation, taking turns to explain the significance of this location. “These three stars are very important to us. Our parents, who have long since gone into the sands of time, named each of us after one of these stars. They said that even though these stars remain close, every star is a sun, unique and individual in its own way. Thank you for leading us back here.” Gain 1 time and 1 morale, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1652.text: The newly freed sentient collapses into your arms, sobbing in relief and gratitude. Gain 1 boost on sentient 1349[/b] and gain 1 morale. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1653.text: With the credits successfully transferred to the tavern, the quest is complete. They even pay you a small bounty out of appreciation. Gain $4, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1654.text: The stone sentient, drawn to the blue crystal, bounds toward you and embraces you in a massive hug. Lose the blue crystal, and gain sentient 1345[/b] and $2. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1656.text: Whether through skill, ingenuity, fate, or fortune, you now have a friggin’ mech suit! Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1657.text: The portals spring to life. Four of them show glimmers of distant locations; the center portal seems programmable for a small sacrifice. Gain destiny 1604[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid, which adds an ENTER[/b] action that you can use only on location 690[/b] (if you do, read entry 1657[/b] in the move[/b] storybook).

1658.text: The metal sentient is overjoyed to see that you procured a one-of-a-kind item, but you have second thoughts about giving it to them. Choose one:

choices.option: Keep the Eye of the Ancient
choices.outcome: Lose this quest.

choices.option: Hand over the Eye of the Ancient
choices.outcome: Lose item 1012[/b] and gain $15. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1659.text: You are successful in driving away the invaders. The sand sentients rejoice in the victory, declaring you their hero. As a reward, they give you $5 and offer you a choice of a time sieve (item 980[/b]) or goggles of futures past (986[/b]), then they escort you from the premises. Go north to 490[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1660.text: The fisherman slowly claps as they admire your catch. “Impressive, very impressive,” they say. “But is it bigger than this?” With a great flourish, they reveal a fish tucked inside their shirt. If you caught a large fish (1226[/b]), you win the competition and gain 2 morale. Otherwise, you lose. Either way, rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1661.text: The gold sentient is elated to have access to such an incredible husk. They explain the significance: “Gold sentients aren’t born with a husk; rather, they all start off more like a golden slug seeking a shell. It’s a coming-of-age process to find the right husk, and it can take multiple husks to get it right. Sometimes it’s a brand-new husk; other times it’s the husk worn by your father and their father before them.” Fill the gold sentient with boost; you also gain $4 and gain 2 boost on your character. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1663.text: As you combine the two shards, you wince as they make contact. When you open your eyes, you find that the shards remain separate, but there is a green energy dancing between them. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1664.text: The sight of you carrying food brings hope to the fire sentients’ gaunt faces. For each boost you remove from a food card to give to the villagers, gain $1 and 1 morale. If you remove at least 4 boost in this way, the sentients give you item 993[/b]. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1665.text: The leaf sentient is relieved and grateful to have arrived at their destination, assuring you that they’re safe from their pursuer here. They give you $5 and a hug (gain 1 morale). Lose sentient 1353[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1666.text: You have found the source of Spor, the contraband substance created from the pollen released into the air by lotuses, collected and processed in this facility. Gain 2 boost on your character, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1667.text: With the blight eradicated, it is as if you can hear the earth itself exhale a great sigh of relief. Gain 1 health, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1668.text: You are elated to be reunited with your cats! The last time you saw them, they were with you in the spaceship as it approached the planet. The kittens act aloof to your advances, but you can tell from the deep rumble of their purrs that they’re happy to see you too. Gain 2 morale, then rotate the quest and add it to your grid.

1669.text: Upon placing the third flag into the ground, you are approached by the elder, assisted by two younger sentients. The elder’s face is running with dusty tears. “Thank you, dearest alien,” they say. “I look forward to watching the wind from the comfort of home while my time on this world comes to an end.” Gain 1 morale, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1670.text: As you stare down the dragon, you know that you have fulfilled your promise to the circuit sentient. What comes next is up to you. Inspired by the legendary creature before you, return 1 challenge die (from anywhere) to the box for the rest of the game (it can be regained later if needed). Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1671.text: The enslaved miners quietly rejoice at their freedom, slipping out into the daylight for the first time in ages. Gain 2 morale, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1672.text: You report back to the lighthouse keeper that the box appears to be some sort of portal, possibly a trap. You encourage them to stay away from it. They thank you with a gift of binoculars (gain item 972[/b]) and $2. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1673.text: Your journey to enlightenment is complete! For each token on this quest, gain 1 random skill. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1677.text: Water sentients gather while you climb, and they cheer when you reach the top before disappearing below the waves. Rotate the quest and add it to your grid.

1678.text: You’re filled with a sense of calm as you gaze upon Moonstone Mountain. Gain 1 morale, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1679.text: After much time and effort, you are the proud parent of a dragon! Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1680.text: The fire merchants are grateful for your help. Not only did you save them from yet another raid, but you also proved to the city watch that they need to increase their security efforts. The merchants give you a custom machete and coins for your service. Gain item 920[/b] and $5, then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1681.text: Fending off the pirate onslaught requires a variety of tricks and maneuvers, but you successfully send every raider back to the sea. The other protectors of the area lead you up a secret staircase and encourage you to touch the Pearl. When you do, your mind goes blank, save for three glowing symbols. Choose life (1236[/b]), spirit (1237[/b]), or mind (1238[/b]). Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid. You may not perform any location actions on 733[/b].

1682.text: Freed from the burden of their debt and ready to make good choices moving forward, the gold sentient pledges their loyalty to you. Gain sentient 1321[/b] with 3 boost on them. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1683.text: As you weave the spell into motion, you direct it toward the ailing circuit sentient to the west. The ground reverberates, and a short time later the sentient comes bounding toward you, filled with vigor. Gain sentient 1314[/b] and $3. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1684.text: The captain is impressed by your range of skills. They trust that their ship is in good hands with you, and share their confidence with the crew. Gain vehicle 1015[/b]. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1685.text: You emerge into the forest, hovershoes in hand, where the jealous shoemaker grabs the shoes from you. They marvel at the craftsmanship of the shoes, and you sense that their envy grows to new heights. Muttering to themselves about the future of footwear, they toss you a bag of coins and scamper away. Gain $10. Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1686.text: You have managed to find one of the most elusive, mysterious, and secret areas on this planet. Rotate the quest and add it to your grid.

1693.text: The landlord, a wood sentient themselves, acts aghast in public at the slaying of their ancestral icon. In private, however, they thank you profusely, elated about the positive impact on housing prices. They say that you’re welcome at any time to take them up on their offer to buy a hill house; in fact, they’ll offer it to you for a discounted price. If you accept, pay $15 and gain structure 1701[/b]. If you reject their offer, they give you some coins as a show of gratitude (gain $10). Rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1694.text: You are successful in breaking through the castle’s defenses, and a wave of water sentients take over the castle. As a reward, they offer you a choice between coins and a fully trained kelp wyrm. Gain animal 1134[/b] or $20. Go east to 460[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1695.text: The fire sentients now have a clear understanding of how the spaceship operates, and they even have some ideas for improvements. They estimate that they can have a brand-new ship ready for you in—they check their numbers— around 100 years. Seeing your shock at that timeframe, they recommend that you head to the desert (southeast of where most fire sentients live), enter the gateway, then trudge a little farther east. It’ll seem like no time at all, they promise. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid; also gain destiny 1607[/b].

1697.text: A discussion ensues between the sentient in the palace and the sentient with whom you’re traveling, then they turn to you to explain the procedure. As far as you can understand, the sentient’s memories and soul will transfer to your body, and yours to theirs. If you have it, you may not gain the benefit of your experience card while Reborn. The change can be reversed later if it isn’t a good fit for either or both of you. If you accept the swap, replace your character card with 1453[/b]. Either way, rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1699.action: Based on the status of the tree, read one

choices.option: You already cut down the tree
choices.outcome: The sentient thanks you for making their job easier, and they proceed to build a boat. Gain vehicle 1015[/b] and keep sentient 1364[/b]. The location actions on 516[/b] are no longer available.

The shipwright is elated and relieved to see the fully grown sea tree. Then they immediately cut it down, chisel it into planks, and start to build a boat. Rotate this quest and add it to your grid. If you lose 1 time, also gain vehicle 1015[/b] and keep sentient 1364[/b] (otherwise, lose the sentient). The location actions on 516[/b] are no longer available.

1700.text: The metal sentient is elated that you found the legendary enchanted forest. They give you a warm meal and some tips on siphoning power before they head into the forest to explore it. Gain 1 health and 1 take[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1702.text: The exhausted farmer is so happy for their blossom hulks to be back home where they belong, and the beasts don’t seem to care either way. In gratitude, the farmer offers you a choice between an untrained blossom hulk (gain animal 841[/b]) or a saddle (gain item 958[/b]). Then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1703.text: You’ve seen other rainbows, but they always disappeared into the horizon—you didn’t realize that any of them had distinct endings. Gain 1 help[/b], then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1704.text: The watchman is overjoyed and relieved to have found the ring. Their job was on the line, and they have a number of goldlings to feed back home. They trust that the ring is in good hands with you if you promise to keep it. Lose sentient 1323[/b] (if you still have them), then rotate this quest and add it to your grid.

1705.text: The roar of engines fills the sky as a rescue-class vessel from your fleet enters the planet’s atmosphere. You figured they would eventually find you. Choose a player to become the active player and perform one of the following actions (view the corresponding storybook for the results):move--HIDE[/b][/b] from the rescue team so you can pursue your new life on this planet (the game does not end).look--NAVIGATE[/b][/b] the rescue vessel toward your location, ending your adventure.engage--GREET[/b][/b] the rescue team with gratitude and open arms, ending your adventure.help--PRAISE[/b][/b] and appreciate the rescue team for their efforts, ending your adventure.take--HIJACK[/b][/b] the vessel so you can continue your adventure on this planet (the game does not end).overpower--TARGET[/b][/b] the rescue ship as it attempts to land so you can achieve victory on your own terms (the game does not end).

174.text: Succeed if you reveal the heights of each wave (second, third, fourth, and fifth) to be 3, 4, 2, and 5 (respectively), as the heights are equal to the number of curves at that top of a wave minus the number of drops emerging from the wave. If you succeed, gain item 1241[/b], an emblem representative of the fire sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the fire curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 1708[/b]. Your location is still 174[/b].

184.text: If you succeed, gain item 1239[/b], an emblem representative of the wind sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the wind curse). If you fail by running out of dice or by not being able to place a die, lose 1 morale. Your location is still 184[/b].

187.text: Succeed if the notes you play are (in order) A, D, C, then G. If you succeed, gain the Horn of Destiny (item 1003[/b]) and perform no other location actions here. If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose the puzzle cards. Your location is still 187[/b].

207.text: Succeed if your path is A1, B1, C2, C3, D4, E4, F3, G2. If you succeed, gain item 1245[/b], an emblem representative of the metal sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the metal curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose the puzzle cards. Your location is still 207[/b].

228.action: engage--TRANSMIT[/b] a signal to your homeworld
228.text: The early days of basic training finally prove useful as you apply the fundamentals of radio signals to the tower. You tap into the tower’s system to send a quick burst that your homeworld should recognize if anyone’s listening. Your rescuers will need to triangulate the signal to find you, but this is a good start. Gain destiny 1598[/b] if you do not already have it. Either way, add 1 signal token to that destiny.

231.text: Combine the cards three times, each forming a unique digit. Succeed if the number you say is 458. If you succeed, gain the Gauntlet of Life (item 1001[/b]) and perform no other location actions here. If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose the puzzle cards. Your location is still 231[/b].

233.text: Succeed if your translation is “12 sparks, 1 fire.” If you succeed, gain item 1243[/b], an emblem representative of the energy sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the energy curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose the puzzle cards. Your location is still 233[/b].

278.text: Mirrored shields glinting in the sunlight, the group of cloaked figures parts as a tall sand sentient glides toward you. “Greetings, wanderer. What business do you have in the north?” Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain 1 health. The guardians escort you west to 277[/b].
Gain 1 morale. The guardians escort you east to 279[/b].
Gain 1 boost. The guardians escort you north to 283[/b].

325.text: If you have animal 817[/b] or 818[/b], you reunite parent and child. The large gilded beetle rips through the golden webs to free their child. They are eternally grateful for your act of kindness, and they present you with a giant blob of gold. Gain item 1185[/b], lose animal 817[/b] or 818[/b], and then go to an adjacent listed location (east, south, or west).

339.text: Succeed if you have the best of the three hands (or tied for the best). If you succeed, gain the Helm of Fortune (item 999[/b]) and perform no other location actions here. If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Your location is still 339[/b].

choices.outcome: First, pay 1 red[/b] to gain 1 yellow[/b] and 1 green[/b]. Second, pay 1 green[/b] to gain 2 yellow[/b] and 1 orange[/b]. Third, pay 3 yellow[/b] to gain 1 green[/b] and 1 red[/b]. Fourth, pay 1 orange[/b] and 1 red[/b] to gain 1 blue[/b]. You should now have 1 green[/b] and 1 blue[/b].

First, pay 1 green[/b] to gain 1 orange[/b] and 1 yellow[/b]. Second, pay 1 orange[/b] to gain 2 red[/b]. Third, pay 1 blue[/b] and 1 red[/b] to gain 2 yellow[/b]. Fourth, pay 2 yellow[/b] to gain 1 purple[/b]. You should now have 1 red[/b], 1 yellow[/b], and 1 purple[/b].

First, pay 1 yellow[/b] to gain 1 red[/b] and 1 orange[/b]. Second, pay 1 orange[/b] and 1 purple[/b] to gain 1 blue[/b] and 2 yellow[/b]. Third, pay 2 yellow[/b] and 1 red[/b] to gain 1 green[/b] and 1 orange[/b]. Fourth, pay 1 green[/b], 1 red[/b], and 1 orange[/b] to gain 1 blue[/b] and 1 purple[/b]. Last, pay 1 purple[/b] and 2 blue[/b] to gain the Gold Emblem.

If you succeed, gain item 1242[/b], an emblem representative of the gold sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the gold curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose the puzzle cards. Your location is still 341[/b].

369.text: When the wood elemental speaks, its long wooden teeth move like piano keys. “You fell for my trap, foolish alien! That pretty box of light and your prized possessions are waiting for you far to the south. I can return you to them if you help me.” Choose one:

choices.option: Release the elemental, setting it free
choices.outcome: The elemental cackles with glee. It picks you up and drops you into the blue aura in the bushes. You black out, and when you awaken, you are back where you started. Go to location 701[/b] and reclaim your possessions, positioning them in your grid as you wish. You may not perform any location actions on 369[/b].

choices.option: Release the elemental, tethering it to you
choices.outcome: The elemental expresses a mix of relief and frustration. Gain elemental 1407[/b]; if you have it, lose the matching elemental curse. You may not perform any location actions here.
Gain 3 boost.

439.text: Succeed if all dice of each type are in their own circle.If you succeed, lose card 802[/b] and gain the Tome of Time (item 1004[/b]), then go to an adjacent location of your choice (north to 440[/b], east to 275[/b], south to 438[/b], west to 159[/b]).If you fail, lose 1 morale, gain 1 random skill, and try again on your next turn with 1 more cube than the previous attempt. Your location is still 439[/b].

511.text: Succeed if you identify at least 5 of the following:The trampled flowersMissing fruit in the basketBroken branches on the small treeAn invisible patch on the upper branch of the big treeThe footprints by the creekThe closed trap doorIf you succeed, gain the Cloak of Invisibility (item 1006[/b]) and perform no other location actions here. If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, you are now on location 511[/b].

537.text: Succeed if the arrangement is D, B, C, A; if so, gain item 1141[/b]. If you fail, lose 1 boost, if possible.

543.text: Succeed if you selected bone A, as this is the only bone where the first legend’s mark contains exactly 3 lines, the second legend’s mark is positioned at the top of the bone, and the third legend’s mark is to the right of the first legend. If you succeed, gain the Bone Harness (item 1007[/b]). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 800[/b]. Either way, exit to 269[/b].

586.text: Succeed if you selected the hook. If you succeed, gain the Tools of Creation (item 997[/b]). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 535[/b]. Your location is still interior 586[/b].

610.text: The mother lets out a cry of joy, rushing toward her calf. After they reunite, the creature expresses its appreciation for you with a wet lick across your face, and it stays by your side, eager to please. Lose animal 810[/b] and gain animal 811[/b]. End your turn; from now on you may not perform any location actions here.

621.text: Succeed if the route is NE, W, S, NW, SW, N, E, SE, NE, W, N. If you succeed, gain item 1240[/b], an emblem representative of the circuit sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the circuit curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 1146[/b]. Your location is still 621[/b].

647.text: Succeed if you win all three battles. If you succeed, gain item 1249[/b], an emblem representative of the water sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the water curse). Succeed or fail, lose card 796[/b]. Your location is still 647[/b].

693.text: Succeed if your image matches this. If you succeed, gain item 1250[/b], an emblem representative of the wood sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the wood curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 536[/b].

708.text: The sentient, seeing their child with you, sweeps both of you into a massive hug. “From the depths of my soulstone, thank you for saving my child. For your courage and bravery, please accept this emblem as an honorary stone sentient.” Lose sentient 1346[/b] and gain item 1247[/b] (also, if you have it, lose the stone curse). The sentient then guides you to a nearby destination of your choice; go to any adjacent location (north is 709[/b], east is 714[/b]).

721.text: See this image for the correct hex. If you succeed, gain item 1247[/b], an emblem representative of the stone sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the stone curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 534[/b]. Your location is still 721[/b].

754.text: There are various possible solutions involving a little luck and clever positioning. Succeed if your token crosses the finish line first. If you succeed, gain item 1246[/b], an emblem representative of the sand sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the sand curse). If you fail, gain 2 random skills. Succeed or fail, lose card 540[/b]. Your location is still 754[/b].

791.text: You have found the Traveler, a child in need of help. It is too risky to remove her from the cryogenic chamber, but you have a few options to complete the rescue mission. Gain destiny 1605[/b] and destiny 1608[/b] (place both in the center). If you choose to pursue destiny 1605[/b], the ship to repair is this specific ship (now or later, return to interior 791[/b] and read the rest of the RESCUE[/b] result). This mission is only complete when you also complete one of these destinies; the actions on the mission card are no longer available.

085.text: Choose one based on the tournament results:

choices.outcome: You’re out of the tournament (lose 1 health but gain ability 1504[/b] plus 1 overpower[/b] per bout you won). Then lose card 797[/b]. Your location is still cave 085[/b].
Gain $15 and learn of a bigger tournament— underwater in a packed arena—due south a long distance from here. Then lose card 797[/b]. Your location is still cave 085[/b].
